title: Festuca broteri
intro: Festuca broteri, also known as broteri's fescue, is a perennial grass species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its slender stems and blue-green foliage. Traditionally, Festuca broteri has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Festuca broteri include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Festuca broteri is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote relaxation, soothe respiratory issues, and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Festuca broteri belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. This plant is further classified as a species within the genus Festuca, which comprises various grass species. More specifically, Festuca broteri is classified as Festuca sect. Festuca, a sub-section within the Festuca genus. It is a perennial grass species native to the Iberian Peninsula. Festuca broteri is classified as Festuca brevis, subsect. Festuca sect. Festuca.
uses: Festuca broteri is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits. The plant is reported to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
